summary: Added a generated-art World Loom panel that turns nearby lane worlds into a cinematic manual selector with level, gate, unlock, avatar, realm, and direct Path/Game/Comms/Asset jumps.

queries: carousel UX guidelines manual control avoid autoplay
3D card carousel CSS performance dashboard UI pattern
cinematic dashboard UI data visualization game world selector
pattern_notes: Manual carousel controls preserve operator pacing and avoid attention theft.
Carousel cards should stay simple, readable, and non-critical; the underlying lane map and list remain the primary navigation.
CSS transforms can add a light 3D feeling without adding a heavy runtime dependency.
Cinematic dashboard surfaces need a clear hierarchy so visual polish does not hide status, gates, or next actions.

generated_asset.prompt: Premium cinematic command-room portal backdrop for a game-like agent-company lane world selector, showing layered holographic world gates, abstract lane portals, subtle depth, polished sci-fi glass surfaces, dark graphite and teal/amber highlights, no text, no logos, no humans, no watermark.

ui_contract: Render World Loom after Atlas Relay and before Progression Constellation.
Use the generated portal-hall system asset as the left-side cinematic visual.
Render at least five lane-world cards around the currently selected lane, with exactly one active card.
Previous and next controls manually rotate selected lane focus without autoplay.
Path, Game, and Comms actions open the selected lane detail views.
ART filters the Visual Asset Vault to system assets and exposes the World Loom portal art.
The panel remains static-site compatible and does not start workers, send commands, or perform external actions.

tdd.red: Playwright behavior check first failed with panel=false, backdrop empty, cards=0, activeCards=0, controls=0, actions=0.
tdd.green: After implementation the same behavior check found the generated backdrop, 7 cards, 1 active card, 2 step controls, 4 actions, and no horizontal overflow.

checks: 390x920 viewport: World Loom rendered one stacked column, 7 lane cards, 1 active card, 4 actions, loaded the generated image, had no clipped panel text, no horizontal overflow, no console errors, and no failed responses.

1280x800 viewport: World Loom rendered image and control column side by side, 7 lane cards, 1 active card, 4 actions, no clipped panel text, no horizontal overflow, no console errors, and no failed responses.
1600x900 viewport: World Loom rendered the wide layout with 7 lane cards, 1 active card, 4 actions, no clipped panel text, no horizontal overflow, no console errors, and no failed responses.
Next control changed selected lane from Platform Engineering to Digital Products Templates Plugins.
PATH action opened the selected lane Path Map.
GAME action opened the selected lane Game tab and rendered a known game surface.
ART action filtered the Asset Vault to system assets and showed World Loom portal hall.
